title: Kafka Architecture
learningObjectives: Name **durability** levers that pair **acks** with **ISR** policy
Diagnose **lag**, **hot keys**, and **metadata** issues using the right metrics
Contrast **Kafka** log semantics with **classic** **queues** for interview clarity
Design **key** strategies that preserve **per-entity** ordering without **skew**

sections.title: Why Kafka Architecture matters
sections.content:
**Kafka** is the **default** answer when teams need **durable**, **replayable** **event streams** at scale. Interviewers expect you to **tie** **brokers**, **partitions**, and **consumer groups** to **real** **failure** modes: **rebalance storms**, **hot partitions**, and **mis-tuned** **poll** intervals.

Strong answers **name** **metrics** (**lag**, **ISR**, **under-replicated partitions**) and **policies** (**acks**, **min ISR**, **retention**). Weak answers **stop** at "**Kafka** is a **queue**" without **ordering** or **parallelism** semantics.

This day frames **Day 59** as **architecture literacy**: how **storage**, **replication**, and **client** behavior fit together before **producer/consumer** deep dives.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Kafka Architecture
sections.content:
### Day 59 — **Kafka** **architecture**

### 1. Topics, partitions, segments
Append-only **log** **segments** per **partition**; **ordering** is **partition-scoped**. Choose **partition count** early with **growth** in mind—**expensive** to shrink.

### 2. Producers and durability hooks
**acks** and **retries** interact with **leader** and **ISR**. **Idempotent** producers address **duplicate** retries at the protocol edge.

### 3. Consumers and groups
**One** **consumer** **instance** per **partition** **max** in a **group**. **Offsets** track **progress**; **commits** define **delivery** semantics with your **processing** code.

### 4. Replication and controllers
**Leaders** serve **reads/writes**; **followers** replicate. **Controller** manages **metadata** and **failover**.

### 5. Operational signals
**Consumer lag**, **under-replicated** **partitions**, **offline** **log directories**, and **rebalance** **rates** are **first-class** **dashboards**.

### 6. 60-second story
**Symptom:** **Lag** after **crash**. **Cause:** **Slow** **poll** + **rebalance** **churn**. **Fix:** **Handler** **SLA** + **tuned** **intervals** + **partition** count **match** parallelism.

sections.title: Basic — Broker, topic, partition map
sections.description: Print core vocabulary for interview whiteboards.
sections.output:
broker   = log storage + leader serving
topic    = named stream
partition = parallelism + ordering unit
replica  = copied log for fault tolerance

sections.title: Intermediate — Partition for key
sections.description: Deterministic partition index from string key.

sections.title: Advanced — Round-robin partition assignment toy
sections.description: Toy assignment: partition index maps to consumer member by modulo.

sections.title: Producers, brokers, consumer group
sections.description: Keys land on partitions; group members share partition assignment.

sections.title: Exercise — Kafka architecture — consumer tuning literacy (Day 59 assignment)
sections.problem:
Match **Day 59** coding drill: print **two lines** **exactly**:
1. **configs:** max.poll.interval.ms, session.timeout.ms, fetch.max.wait.ms
2. **metric:** consumer-lag per partition + rebalance rate
hints: No extra spaces around colons beyond the template.
Name one **metric** and one **config** you would check on-call for this day’s topic.

sections.title: Use cases — Kafka Architecture
sections.content:
## Use cases — **Kafka architecture**

### 1) E-commerce **order** **events** backbone
**Context:** Checkout emits **order.created**, **payment.captured**, **shipment.ready** for analytics, search, and fraud.
**Why Day 59:** Partition and **consumer-group** design decides **per-order** ordering vs **fan-out** throughput.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Choose **partition key** = `orderId` for order-scoped ordering.
2. Size **partition count** for peak **write** TPS and **consumer** parallelism.
3. Run **three** consumer groups (analytics, search, fraud) — each reads the **same** topic independently.
4. Dashboard **consumer lag** per **partition** + **under-replicated partitions**.
**Failure hook:** **Hot** **merchant** keys → one **partition** lags while others are idle.

### 2) **Microservice** **changelog** **fan-out**
**Context:** A **core** service publishes **entity** updates to many downstreams.
**Why Day 59:** Brokers + **replication** must survive **AZ** loss without silent **durability** loss.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Set **replication.factor** and **min.insync.replicas** for **money** paths (`acks=all`).
2. Enable **rack awareness** so replicas span **AZs**.
3. Document **unclean leader election** policy per **topic tier**.
**Failure hook:** **ISR** shrink → producers **block**; alert on **min ISR** breaches.

### 3) **Data** **platform** **ingestion** **SLA**
**Context:** Thousands of **topics** owned by product teams feeding the **warehouse**.
**Why Day 59:** **Metadata** storms and **rebalance** churn show up as **p99** **lag** spikes.
**Implementation outline:**
1. Standardize **consumer** configs: **max.poll.interval.ms**, **session.timeout.ms**, **fetch.max.wait.ms**.
2. Cap **per-topic** **throughput** in **ADR**; **reject** unbounded **retention** on **shared** clusters.
3. Run **game days**: **broker** **bounce**, **consumer** **crash**, **network** **partition**.
**Failure hook:** **Rebalance** storm after deploy → **handler** time exceeded **max.poll.interval**.

### 4) **Platform** **on-call** **playbook**
**Context:** Pager fires on **lag** and **offline** **log** **directories**.
**Why Day 59:** You must read **cluster** topology faster than tailing random logs.
**Implementation outline:**
1. First chart: **lag** by **partition**; second: **ISR** and **leader** **election** rate.
2. Correlate with **deploy** times and **consumer** **generation** changes.
3. Escalate **broker** **disk** / **controller** issues with **runbook** links.
**Failure hook:** Mis-blaming **“Kafka slow”** when **downstream** **DB** is the **bottleneck**.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a **Kafka broker** and **cluster** role?
conceptual.answer: **Brokers** persist **topics** as **append-only** logs; a **cluster** shares metadata via the **controller** and replicates **partitions** for availability. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is a **Kafka broker** and **cluster** role?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why **partition** a **topic**?
conceptual.answer: **Partitions** are the **unit of parallelism** and **storage shard**; **ordering** is **only per partition**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Why **partition** a **topic**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: How does **key-based partitioning** work?
conceptual.answer: By default the **partitioner** hashes the **record key** modulo **partition count** so the **same key** maps to the **same partition**. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **How does **key-based partitioning** work?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is the **controller** broker?
conceptual.answer: One broker **coordinates** **leader** elections, **ISR** maintenance, and **metadata** updates; **loss** triggers **fast** **failover**. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is the **controller** broker?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **ISR**?
conceptual.answer: **In-sync replicas**: followers **caught up** enough to **promote** if the **leader** fails. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **ISR**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **replication.factor** vs **min.insync.replicas**?
conceptual.answer: **RF** controls **copies**; **min.insync.replicas** gates **producer durability** when using **acks=all**. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is **replication.factor** vs **min.insync.replicas**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why avoid **hot partitions**?
conceptual.answer: One **partition** absorbs **skewed keys** and becomes a **throughput** and **storage** bottleneck. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Why avoid **hot partitions**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is a **consumer group**?
conceptual.answer: A **logical** set of consumers that **share** **partition assignment**; **each partition** is read by **at most one** member in the group. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What is a **consumer group**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What happens on **consumer crash**?
conceptual.answer: The **group coordinator** **rebalances**; partitions **move** to **survivors**; **uncommitted** work may be **redelivered** under **at-least-once**. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What happens on **consumer crash**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What are **offsets**?
conceptual.answer: **Monotonic positions** per **partition**; **committed offsets** define **progress** in the **consumer group**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **What are **offsets**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Difference **log retention** and **compaction**?
conceptual.answer: **Retention** **time/size** deletes **old** segments; **compaction** keeps the **latest** value **per key** for **changelog** topics. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Difference **log retention** and **compaction**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **ZooKeeper** vs **KRaft**?
conceptual.answer: Modern clusters use **KRaft** for **metadata**; legacy used **ZooKeeper**—interviews may still mention **migration**. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why **rack awareness**?
conceptual.answer: **Replica placement** across **racks/AZs** reduces **correlated** failure wiping **ISR**. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Why **rack awareness**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: What is **unclean leader election**?
conceptual.answer: Promoting a **non-ISR** replica **risks** **data loss**—usually **disabled** for **critical** topics. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: How do **metadata** requests affect clients?
conceptual.answer: **Clients** cache **topic/partition leaders**; **leader movement** triggers **metadata refresh** and **NOT_LEADER** handling. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **How do **metadata** requests affect clients?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: When does **adding partitions** fail to reduce **end-to-end lag**?
conceptual.answer: **Lag** is bounded by the **slowest** stage: **hot partitions**, **single-threaded** processing, **downstream** bottlenecks, or **not enough consumers** to match partitions. More partitions without fixing skew or consumer capacity can **increase** operational overhead without improving p99.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **When does **adding partitions** fail to reduce **end-to-end lag**?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.question: Which configuration or cluster policy most often invalidates a textbook answer?
followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

conceptual.question: Why is **per-partition ordering** weaker than many teams assume?
conceptual.answer: Ordering holds **only within one partition**. Cross-partition order is **undefined** unless you engineer **single partition**, **versioned** state, or **downstream** reconciliation. Interviewers probe whether you confuse **topic** ordering with **global** ordering.

followUps.question: What production metric would you watch first to validate that answer?
followUps.answer: For **Why is **per-partition ordering** weaker than many teams assume?**, pair **consumer lag per partition**, **under-replicated partitions**, and **request/queue time on brokers** with **traces** from producing services. Alert on sustained lag growth and ISR shrinkage, not only error rates.

followUps.answer: **min.insync.replicas** vs **replication.factor**, **unclean leader election**, **retention/compaction** on money topics, and **max.poll.interval.ms** vs handler runtime usually surface gaps between slides and production.

codeBased.question: Name three producer **acks** modes and durability trade-off.
codeBased.answer: // acks=0 fire-and-forget; acks=1 leader only; acks=all ISR ack

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// acks=0 fire-and-forget; acks=1 leader only; acks=all ISR ack** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: Consumer **subscribe** vs **assign**.
codeBased.answer: // subscribe: group rebalance; assign: static partition map, no auto balance

followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// subscribe: group rebalance; assign: static partition map, no auto balance** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: **max.poll.interval.ms** meaning.
codeBased.answer: // max time between poll() calls before consumer considered dead

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// max time between poll() calls before consumer considered dead** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: **session.timeout.ms** vs **heartbeat**.
codeBased.answer: // member liveness to coordinator; heartbeats keep session alive

followUps.question: What failure mode shows up first when this is misconfigured under load?
followUps.answer: Typical symptoms: **rebalance storms**, **duplicate delivery** after retries, **metadata refresh loops** (**NOT_LEADER**), or **producer buffer exhaustion**. Tie **// member liveness to coordinator; heartbeats keep session alive** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.question: How would you prove correctness in staging without flaky integration tests?
followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

codeBased.question: In Java, why is `Math.floorMod(key.hashCode(), numPartitions)` only a teaching toy?
codeBased.answer: Real partitioners use the **client** implementation and may **murmur** hash or differ by version; `hashCode` **stability** across JVM versions is not a broker contract. Use the **producer** API and tests rather than reimplementing internals.

codeBased.question: What does `NOT_LEADER_FOR_PARTITION` imply for a producer retry loop?
codeBased.answer: **Metadata** is stale: **leader** moved. **Refresh** metadata and retry with backoff—avoid **tight** loops that **amplify** load. Also verify **cluster** health and **client** timeouts.

Tie ****Metadata** is stale: **leader** moved. **Refresh** metadata and retry with backoff—avoid **tight**** to observable signals before jumping to code changes.

followUps.answer: Use **deterministic** workloads: fixed keys, controlled partition counts, inject broker pause/leader movement in a test cluster, and assert **offset** progress + **exactly-once** expectations with idempotent sinks or test harnesses.

seniorScenario.question: **Incident:** **Consumer lag** explodes after **deploy**; **rebalance storm** in logs.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Freeze deploy; snapshot **rebalance reason**.

**(2) Root cause** **max.poll.interval** exceeded due to **slow handler** + **larger batches**.

**(3) Fix** Fix **handler** time or **tune** intervals; **cooperative** rebalance.

**(4) Prevention** Alert on **rebalance rate** per group. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Incident:** **Consumer lag** explodes after **deploy**; **rebalance storm** in logs.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Design:** **Global ordering** for **all orders** in Kafka.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Challenge requirement.

**(2) Root cause** Single partition **serializes** throughput.

**(3) Fix** Use **orderId** partition key; accept **per-key** ordering only.

**(4) Prevention** Document **SLO** vs **ordering** trade-off. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads. When **Kubernetes** is involved, cite **readiness**, **HPA**, **NetworkPolicy**, and **Service** **DNS** as factors that change **latency** independent of **Java** **code**. For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations.

followUps.question: How do you communicate blast radius and ETA to stakeholders during the incident?
followUps.answer: For ****Design:** **Global ordering** for **all orders** in Kafka.**, report **which topics/partitions** lag, **whether writes are still accepted**, **data-loss risk** (ISR), and **consumer backlog time**. Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

followUps.question: What permanent guardrails prevent a repeat in six months?
followUps.answer: Add **SLOs** on lag and ISR, **game days** for AZ loss and broker restarts, **capacity models** for partitions, and **review gates** for retention/compaction and ACL changes.

seniorScenario.question: **Ops:** **Under-replicated partitions** during **AZ failure**.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Check **ISR** and **rack** placement.

**(2) Root cause** **RF** or **min ISR** misconfigured for **AZ** loss.

**(3) Fix** Raise **RF**, verify **rack awareness**, **pause** risky **traffic**.

**(4) Prevention** Game-day **AZ** drill. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

Give a **bounded** ETA tied to a concrete mitigation (scale, pause traffic, fail over).

seniorScenario.question: **Scale:** **12 partitions**, **40 consumers** in one group.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Explain **idle consumers**.

**(2) Root cause** **Max parallelism** = **partition count** for the group.

**(3) Fix** **Repartition** topic or **multiple** groups for **read scaling**.

**(4) Prevention** Capacity model in **ADR**. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** DLQ pattern.

**(2) Root cause** No **skip** path; **offset** stuck.

**(3) Fix** **Tombstone** handling + **DLQ** + **manual** intervention playbook.

**(4) Prevention** Test **poison** in **staging**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

seniorScenario.question: **Cost:** **Retention** **7 years** on **high-volume** topic.
seniorScenario.answer:
**(1) Immediate response** Tiered storage or topic split.

**(2) Root cause** Disk and **replay** cost **unsustainable**.

**(3) Fix** Compaction + **export** to **object storage** + **governance**.

**(4) Prevention** Finance sign-off on **retention** policy. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

wrongAnswers: **Kafka** guarantees **global** **ordering** across a topic — **Correction:** **Per-partition** ordering only.
**More consumers** than **partitions** speeds up **one** group — **Correction:** **Extra** members **idle**.
**acks=1** is always **enough** for **payments** — **Correction:** **Durability** needs **acks=all** + **min ISR** design.
**Offsets** are **per topic** only — **Correction:** **Per** **partition** per consumer group.
**Delete topic** instantly frees **all** disk — **Correction:** **Segments** and **replicas** recover **at** **pace** of **GC**.
**Replication** fixes **hot keys** — **Correction:** **Same** partition still **hot**; need **key** design.
**Consumer** **threads** **unlimited** help — **Correction:** **Poll loop** and **partition** count bound useful work.

sections.title: MCQ Quiz — Kafka Architecture
sections.description: Thirty questions from basic to advanced — Kafka Architecture. Read every option; distractors are plausible but wrong for a precise reason.

questions.question: [Kafka Architecture] Which statement best describes **broker cluster** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **broker cluster** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Architecture**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Architecture] Which option is a **plausible distractor** interviewers use about **partition ordering**?

options.A: A precise, bounded statement tied to ordering, ISR, or consumer groups.
options.B: A blanket claim that ignores partition boundaries or client semantics.
options.C: A metric-backed troubleshooting step.
options.D: A configuration paired with a failure mode.

questions.explanation: Watch for absolutes on **partition ordering** that ignore **per-partition** semantics and client responsibilities.

questions.question: [Kafka Architecture] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **consumer group**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **consumer group** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Kafka Architecture] Which statement best describes **ISR** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **ISR** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Architecture**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

questions.question: [Kafka Architecture] You see misconfiguration around **replication factor** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **replication factor** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Kafka Architecture] You see misconfiguration around **follower replication** in a code review. What is the best first response?

options.A: Map settings to infrastructure policy, then reproduce under load in a controlled environment.
options.B: Delete the topic and recreate it with fewer partitions.
options.C: Set all timeouts to 1ms for safety.
options.D: Disable metrics to reduce noise.

questions.explanation: Validate **follower replication** against defaults, workload, and symptoms (lag, errors, consistency) before changing code.

questions.question: [Kafka Architecture] Incident: user-visible delay worsens after a deploy touching **leader epoch**. What is a credible first hypothesis?

options.A: The deploy changed timing, batching, retries, or resource limits along the critical path.
options.B: The system randomly deleted all state.
options.C: DNS always causes delay; ignore application logic.
options.D: You always need fewer threads when latency grows.

questions.explanation: Tie **leader epoch** to measurable regressions: compare golden signals before/after and narrow to the changed surface.

questions.question: [Kafka Architecture] Which statement best describes **partition count** in production Kafka/messaging contexts?

options.A: It is a core lever teams tune with metrics, failure modes, and SLOs in mind.
options.B: It is purely a UI concern unrelated to brokers or clients.
options.C: It disables all replication automatically.
options.D: It guarantees global total order for every topic.

questions.explanation: **partition count** belongs to the operational model for **Kafka Architecture**—tie it to trade-offs and measurements, not absolutes.

sections.title: Kafka Architecture — cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Rule of thumb | Interview one-liner |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Partition | Ordering + parallelism unit | Ordering + parallelism unit |
| Consumer group | Shared assignment; max 1 reader/partition | Shared assignment; max 1 reader/partition |
| acks all | Waits for ISR policy | Waits for ISR policy |
| Lag | High-water minus consumer position | High-water minus consumer position |
| Rebalance | Membership / poll timeout driven | Membership / poll timeout driven |
| ISR | In-sync follower set | In-sync follower set |
| Controller | Metadata + leader elections | Metadata + leader elections |
| Retention | Time/size; compaction optional | Time/size; compaction optional |
| Key hash | Stable routing for entity ordering | Stable routing for entity ordering |
| Hot key | Skew → single partition overload | Skew → single partition overload |
| Operational metric | Watch lag + ISR + request latency | Say lag per partition and under-replicated partitions |
| Failure drill | Broker bounce + consumer crash chaos | Prove rebalance and recovery SLOs in staging |
| Capacity | Partitions bound parallelism per group | More consumers than partitions stay idle |
| Security | ACLs least privilege per principal | Separate prod cluster or strict topic governance |
| Cost | Retention drives disk and replay cost | Tiered storage or export before cutting retention |
